candidates.name: Steiny
candidates.rationale: From 'stein' (the iconic beer mug) softened to a friendly, sayable 6-letter name. Instantly reads 'beer' to a global audience without spelling out a beer style, so it stays inclusive across lagers/ales/craft. Warm, gamification-friendly (steins as a points/streak currency writes itself), easy to say in most languages, strong App Store title. Vowel-heavy so it trends and pronounces cleanly.
candidates.risks: 'Stein' is also a very common surname (and 'Steiny' a nickname), so it's a somewhat generic token; trademark clearance in class 9/42 needed. Slight cutesy diminutive vibe. German-culture connotation may feel Oktoberfest-specific to some.
candidates.availability_notes: Searches for 'Steinly'/'Stein' beer apps returned only breweries, distributors and beer gardens (Stein Distributing, Steins Beer Garden, Stein Brewing) — no consumer beer app named Stein/Steiny/Steinly. 'Steinly.app' search returned nothing. steiny.com/.app likely need checking but the app-name lane looks open. Sources: steinbeer.com, steinsbeergarden.com, stein-brewing.com, en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beer_stein

candidates.name: Brewt
candidates.rationale: A tight, invented 5-letter mashup of 'brew' + 'brut/brute' that looks modern and ownable (the missing vowel reads as intentional, startup-native branding). Keeps the universal 'brew' root so it's globally legible as beer, while being distinctive enough to trademark and dominate search. Verb-able ('brewt it', 'get brewted') for social/gamified UX.
candidates.risks: Silent/ambiguous pronunciation ('brute' vs 'brew-t') can confuse first-time users and hurt word-of-mouth. 'Brute' association is slightly aggressive. Close to many 'brew' brands (Breww, Brewfather, Brewee) so it lives in a crowded phonetic neighborhood even if the exact string is free.
candidates.availability_notes: No app or site called 'Brewt' found — 'Brewt's' (brewtsmix.com) is a cocktail-mix brand, not a beer app; adjacent names Breww (brewery software), Brewfather, Brewee, Brewly (coffee) exist but none is 'Brewt'. The .app/.com exact string appears unclaimed by an app. Sources: brewtsmix.com, breww.com, brewfather.app, brewee.app, apps.apple.com (Brewly Coffee)

candidates.name: Pilsy
candidates.rationale: Derived from 'pilsner' — the world's most-brewed beer style — clipped to a playful 5-letter name. Reads unmistakably 'beer', is fun and warm without being cringe, and is short enough to be a punchy App Store title and hashtag. Coined enough to be trademarkable and to leave .com/.app open.
candidates.risks: Pilsner is a style, which could subtly imply the app is lager-only rather than all beer. 'Pils' is less familiar as a word in the US than in Europe. Cutesy '-y' ending competes tonally with Steiny. Needs style-neutral marketing to avoid pigeonholing.
candidates.availability_notes: Searches for 'Pilsn'/'Pils' beer apps surfaced only AI beer-name generators and unrelated brewing software — no consumer app named Pilsy/Pilsn found. Appears wide open as an app name. Sources: domainwheel.com/beer-name-generator, beerandbrewing.com/tools/beer-name-generator

candidates.name: Clink
candidates.rationale: The universal sound and gesture of a toast — inherently social, celebratory and language-agnostic, which fits the friends/flights/gamified brief perfectly. 5 letters, hard-consonant snap, extremely easy to say worldwide and highly evocative of the 'cheers' moment. Great verb and notification hook ('you got a Clink from Sam').
candidates.risks: Not beer-specific (works for any drink), so it leans social over category-owning. Real collisions exist in the broader drinks space: Heineken's 2026 'Clinker' wearable and 'Clink Beverage Co.' — both could muddy search and raise trademark questions even though neither is a beer-logging app.
candidates.availability_notes: No beer-logging app named 'Clink' found. Nearby: Heineken 'Clinker' (a 2026 marketing wearable) and 'Clink Beverage Co.' (a canned-cocktail brand). Neither occupies the App Store beer-app lane, but both warrant a trademark check. Sources: thedrum.com (Heineken Clinker), thesobrmarket.com/blogs/brands/clink

candidates.name: Froth
candidates.rationale: The foamy head on a pour — pure, vivid beer imagery in one real 5-letter English word. Punchy, premium-sounding, easy to say globally, and it evokes hype/excitement ('frothing') which suits a social, gamified vibe. Strong logo potential (the foam line).
candidates.risks: Biggest risk of the set: 'Froth' is already a LIVE app on both the iOS App Store and Google Play — a surf-report app (bundle ca.craver.froth) — so the exact App Store name is effectively taken and would force a modifier ('Froth Beer'). Also a common brewery/beer name (Froth Brewing Co., Matilda Bay 'Frothy'). High collision.
candidates.availability_notes: CONFIRMED collision: 'Froth' exists as a shipping app on apps.apple.com/us/app/froth/id6447825845 and play.google.com (ca.craver.froth), plus Froth Brewing Co. (Buffalo) and 'Frothy' beer. Not recommended unless paired with a distinct suffix. Sources: apps.apple.com/us/app/froth/id6447825845, craverapp.com/apps/froth, frothbrewing.com

candidates.name: Suds
candidates.rationale: Beloved slang for beer, warm and unpretentious, 4 letters, instantly understood in English-speaking markets and fun to say. Plural feel suits a 'rounds with friends' social product; playful without being cringe. Good for a casual, community-first brand.
candidates.risks: English-slang-dependent, so it loses meaning in non-English markets (weak for a global app). Also literally means soap suds, a possible off-brand read. Beer-brand uses exist. 'Suds' is generic, so trademark distinctiveness is weak and search is noisy.
candidates.availability_notes: No dominant consumer beer app named 'Suds' found — only a 'SUDS' beer by Palm City Brewing (on Untappd), old brewing software references, and slang articles. App lane looks open but the term is generic. Sources: untappd.com/b/palm-city-brewing-suds, stltoday.com (‘new beer app puts suds in your hands’), quora.com/Why-do-people-call-beer-SUDS

candidates.name: Tappr
candidates.rationale: From the beer 'tap' + the check-in 'tap' gesture — a double meaning that nails the core interaction (tap to check in a beer). 5 letters, dropped-vowel startup styling, easy to say, and beer-native without naming a style. Natural verb for the whole product ('tap it in').
candidates.risks: Phonetically and conceptually very close to Untappd, the incumbent — could read as derivative or invite confusion/legal attention from the category leader. Dropped-vowel names are a dated trend. 'Tapper' is also an arcade/beer game, so some heritage collision.
candidates.availability_notes: Searches for 'Tapd'/'Taproom' apps found no dominant app named Tappr/Tapd (only Untappd, TapHunter, Taplister, Trademark Brewing). Exact 'Tappr' appears free in beer, but proximity to Untappd is the real concern, not string availability. Sources: untappd.com, taphunter.com, twitter.com/Taplister

candidates.name: Nectar
candidates.rationale: Poetic, premium framing of beer as 'the nectar' — aspirational, sensory and inclusive across all drink types, which supports future expansion beyond beer. A real word, easy to pronounce globally, and it signals quality/taste for a rate-and-discover product. Elegant, non-cringe.
candidates.risks: Not beer-specific and heavily used across food/drink/wellness (juices, hard-nectar seltzers, honey, many apps/brands named Nectar in other categories), so trademark and SEO are crowded and .com is almost certainly long gone. Reads slightly upscale/wine-y rather than beer-casual.
candidates.availability_notes: No dedicated beer app named 'Nectar' found, but the word is widely used commercially (e.g., 'Jumex Hard Nectar' malt beverage) and across unrelated app categories (mattresses, loyalty, etc.), so it's crowded even if the beer-app slot is open. Sources: totalwine.com (Jumex Hard Nectar), facebook.com/americastestkitchen (‘beer… the nectar…’)

candidates.name: Pinta
candidates.rationale: Spanish/Italian/Portuguese for 'pint' — a genuinely global take on the beer 'pint' that plays in the huge Spanish- and Latin-American beer markets while staying legible to English speakers. 5 letters, open vowels, very easy to say worldwide; friendlier and more ownable than the crowded 'Pint'.
candidates.risks: Adjacent apps already exist: 'Pinta Libre' (beer/coffee perks) and the crowded 'Pint' family (Pint Please, Pint Post). Possible confusion with those, and 'Pinta' is also a place name/columbus-ship reference. Trademark distinctiveness vs. Pinta Libre must be cleared.
candidates.availability_notes: 'Pinta Libre' is a live App Store app (beer & coffee perks), and 'Pint Please'/'Pint Post' dominate the 'pint' space — so 'Pinta' as a bare name is close to existing marks. Open-ish but adjacent-crowded. Sources: apps.apple.com/us/app/pinta-libre-beer-coffee/id1425845526, pintplease.com, pintpost.app

candidates.name: Hoply
candidates.rationale: A coined, brand-safe cousin of 'hops' + a friendly '-ly' — clearly beer (hops) yet distinct from the taken 'Hoppy'/'HOPS' apps. 5 letters, easy to say, modern SaaS-style name that's trademarkable and likely leaves .com/.app open. Works as a verb-ish social handle.
candidates.risks: 'Hops' skews IPA/craft, which can feel less inclusive of lager/stout drinkers globally. Only one letter off 'Hoppy' (a live app) and near 'HOPS' (a live social beer app), so audible confusion and trademark-similarity risk are real. '-ly' naming is a bit played out.
candidates.availability_notes: 'Hoppy' (AI beer search, apps.apple.com/us/app/hoppy-ai-powered-beer-search/id1406526158) and 'HOPS' (Google Play social beer network, com.hops.beer.app) both exist; no exact 'Hoply' app found, but it sits phonetically between two live beer apps. Sources: apps.apple.com/us/app/hoppy-ai-powered-beer-search/id1406526158, play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.hops.beer.app

candidates.name: Cheerz
candidates.rationale: The single most universal beer/social word on earth ('cheers'), respelled with a 'z' for ownability and a modern edge. Instantly warm, celebratory, and understood in virtually every market — ideal for a social + flights + gamified product built around toasting friends. Great as a reaction/notification ('send a Cheerz').
candidates.risks: 'Cheerz' with a Z is already used by a photo-printing brand (Cheerz) in Europe, creating cross-category trademark noise. The word 'cheers' is generic and heavily used, so distinctiveness is thin and search is noisy. Not beer-specific (works for any drink or toast).
candidates.availability_notes: No beer-logging app named 'Cheerz' found; a 'Cheers' cocktail app (cheersmobileapp.com) and 'Cheers!' drinking-game app exist, and 'Cheerz' is a known photo-print company — so beer-app lane is open but the mark is contested cross-category. Sources: cheersmobileapp.com, apps.apple.com/us/app/cheers-fun-beer-drinking-game/id1076227368

data.top_pick_rationale: Steiny is the strongest balance of the brief's constraints. It is short (6 letters), phonetically simple, and pronounceable in nearly every language via open vowels — critical for a 'free global' app. It reads 'beer' instantly through the universally recognizable stein/mug without locking into a single beer style (unlike Pilsy, Hoply, or Barley-type names), which keeps it inclusive for a worldwide catalog of lagers, ales, and craft. Crucially, it survived collision research: searches for stein/steinly beer apps returned only breweries, distributors, and beer gardens — no consumer beer app owns this name, so the App Store title lane and likely the .app/.com feel are open, unlike Froth (a live cross-category app), Pint/Hoppy/Swig/Bevvy (all taken in beer/alcohol), or Tappr/Cheerz (confusingly close to incumbents Untappd and existing 'Cheers' apps). It is warm and playful without being cringe, and the stein maps naturally onto the gamified layer — steins as points, streaks, flights, and badges. Recommended next steps before commit: a formal USPTO/EUIPO trademark search in Nice classes 9 and 42, and domain checks on steiny.app / steiny.com / getsteiny.com. If 'Steiny' reads too much like a nickname for the target brand tone, Brewt is the best invented-name fallback (fully clear as an app string), and Pilsy the best warm/ownable fallback.
data.details:
Research method: I ran WebSearch across the 2026 beer-app landscape (Untappd alternatives, social beer apps) and then collision-tested ~25 candidate strings against the App Store, Google Play, breweries, and drink brands.

Verified TAKEN / crowded in beer or alcohol apps (avoid): Untappd (incumbent), Pint Please, Hoppy (AI beer search, live iOS), HOPS (social beer, live Android), Swig (social craft-drink app), Bevvy/Bevy (whisky companion + London alcohol delivery), Frothy ('Frothy Beer Venues', live iOS), Fizz ('Fizz: Alcohol and Snacks', live iOS), Malt ('Maltapp'), Pint Post, Pinta Libre, Beer.Social, CRAFT, Brewee, Breww, Brewfather, BeerTasting, RateBeer, BeerAdvocate.

Verified app-name collision but NOT beer (still blocks the App Store title): Froth (live surf app on iOS + Google Play, ca.craver.froth) and Brewly (a coffee app).

Verified relatively CLEAR as beer-app names (recommended lane): Steiny/Steinly (only breweries/distributors found), Brewt (only a cocktail-mix brand 'Brewt's'), Pilsy/Pilsn (nothing found), Hoply (exact string free, but sits between live 'Hoppy' and 'HOPS'). Contested cross-category: Clink (Heineken 'Clinker' 2026 + 'Clink Beverage Co.'), Cheerz (photo-print brand + existing 'Cheers' cocktail/drinking-game apps), Nectar (heavily used across drinks and unrelated app categories), Suds (generic slang, a Palm City 'SUDS' beer exists).

Caveat on availability: WebSearch cannot authoritatively confirm live .com/.app domain registration or trademark status — those need a registrar lookup and a USPTO/EUIPO/WIPO search. The 'availability_notes' reflect app-store and web brand collisions only. Treat every top candidate as 'clear the App Store name; still run formal TM + domain checks before committing.'

Key sources: drinkist.app/blog/untappd-alternative; top10.com/apps-for-beer-lovers; beer.social; apps.apple.com/us/app/craft-the-social-beer-app/id6444923219; apps.apple.com/us/app/pint-please-beer-ratings/id833522363; apps.apple.com/us/app/hoppy-ai-powered-beer-search/id1406526158; play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.hops.beer.app; stgermaincellars.com/blog/swig (Swig); apps.apple.com/us/app/froth/id6447825845 + craverapp.com/apps/froth (Froth surf app collision); apps.apple.com/au/app/frothy-beer-venues/id6450882298; apps.apple.com/us/app/fizz-alcohol-and-snacks/id6741012644; bevvy.com / bevy.co; brewtsmix.com; steinbeer.com / steinsbeergarden.com / en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beer_stein; thedrum.com (Heineken Clinker 2026); pintpost.app; apps.apple.com/us/app/pinta-libre-beer-coffee/id1425845526.

key_findings: FRAMEWORK VERDICT: Native Swift/SwiftUI for the iOS-first launch. The app's differentiators are exactly where native wins in 2026: label/beer scanning (VisionKit DataScanner + AVFoundation get WWDC APIs day-one; RN wrappers lag 3-12 months), a fluid social feed (SwiftUI + ProMotion 120Hz, native scroll/gesture on complex media lists), premium feel (Dynamic Island, Live Activities for 'friends checking in nearby', haptics), CoreLocation geofencing/background location, and MapKit. Expo/RN's New Architecture (Fabric/JSI/TurboModules, mandatory since RN 0.82) has closed the raw-perf gap for CRUD apps, but not the platform-integration or polish gap. Since the account is iOS-first with Apple Developer ready and there is no stated Android deadline, cross-platform is a premature optimization. Revisit RN/Expo only if/when Android becomes a hard requirement AND the team is JS-heavy.

AUTH: Use Supabase Auth native flow, NOT web/OAuth redirect. Sign in with Apple via ASAuthorizationController returns an identityToken (JWT); pass it to supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken({provider:'apple', token: identityToken, nonce: rawNonce}). Apple requires the request's nonce to be SHA256-hashed while the raw nonce is sent to Supabase. Google Sign-In (GoogleSignIn-iOS SDK) returns an idToken passed the same way. Supabase validates against Apple/Google public keys and links accounts by email. iOS-only means you can skip Apple Services ID/Secret key config entirely.

APPLE COMPLIANCE (the make-or-break constraint): This must be a discovery/social/logging app, NEVER a commerce app. Guideline 1.4.3 forbids apps that 'encourage excessive alcohol consumption' or 'facilitate the sale of controlled substances/tobacco' — do not add ordering, delivery, or buy buttons at launch (that path invites rejection and is the single biggest risk). Set age rating to 17+ honestly (Guideline 2.3.6: mis-rating triggers regulator inquiry). Add an in-app declared-age gate on first launch (Guideline 1.2.1 mechanism), a responsible-drinking disclaimer, and no content targeting minors.

UGC MODERATION IS MANDATORY, not optional (Guideline 1.2): check-ins, photos, ratings, and comments are user-generated, so the app MUST ship with (1) a content filter/EULA-agree gate, (2) a report mechanism, (3) block-user capability, and (4) published contact info — on day one. Rejection under 1.2 is common for social apps that launch without all four.

GEOSPATIAL: Use the PostGIS extension (enable in Supabase). Store venue location as `geography(Point,4326)`, add a GiST index, and expose a SECURITY DEFINER RPC using ST_DWithin (indexed radius filter, meters) + ST_Distance (exact sort). 'Popular near me' = the same nearby query joined against a check-in count over a recent window, ranked by a blend of distance and recent activity.

STORAGE + PUSH: Supabase Storage (S3-backed) with a private `check-in-photos` bucket, RLS on storage.objects keyed to the uploader's user_id in the path, and built-in image transforms (Pro plan) for feed thumbnails vs full-res. Push notifications require an external service — Supabase does not send APNs directly. Store the APNs device token per install; a Supabase Edge Function (triggered by DB webhook on new follow/check-in/badge) calls APNs directly with a .p8 auth key (JWT), avoiding a Firebase dependency.
recommendations: MVP STACK: Native Swift 6 / SwiftUI (iOS 18+), Supabase (Postgres + PostGIS + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions). Auth: Sign in with Apple + Google via signInWithIdToken. supabase-swift SDK for data/auth/storage. This is the whole backend — no separate app server needed at MVP.

MVP SCHEMA (public schema, RLS on every table): profiles(id uuid PK ref auth.users, username unique, display_name, avatar_url, age_verified bool, created_at); breweries(id, name, country, city, website, logo_url); beers(id, brewery_id FK, name, style, abv numeric, ibu int, description, label_url); venues(id, name, address, location geography(Point,4326), category); check_ins(id, user_id FK, beer_id FK, venue_id FK nullable, rating numeric(2,1) check 0.5..5, comment, photo_path, location geography(Point,4326), created_at, is_flagged bool default false); badges(id, slug, name, description, icon_url, criteria jsonb); user_badges(user_id, badge_id, earned_at, PK(user_id,badge_id)); friendships(requester_id, addressee_id, status enum pending/accepted/blocked, PK(requester_id,addressee_id)); follows(follower_id, followee_id, PK pair); flights(id, user_id FK, name, notes, created_at) + flight_items(flight_id, beer_id, position); reports(id, reporter_id, target_type, target_id, reason, status, created_at); device_tokens(user_id, apns_token, platform, updated_at). Enable PostGIS; GiST indexes on venues.location and check_ins.location.

RLS POLICY SHAPE: profiles readable by all authed users, writable only by owner. check_ins/ratings insertable by owner, readable by owner + accepted friends/followees (or public per privacy flag), soft-deletable by owner; flagged rows hidden from feed. friendships/follows mutate only rows where auth.uid() is a participant. reports insert-only for reporters; moderation reads via service role. device_tokens owner-only.

GEO RPC (MVP): create RPC nearby_venues(lat,lng,radius_m) returning venues + distance via ST_DWithin/ST_Distance ordered by distance. Add popular_near_me(lat,lng,radius_m,since interval) = nearby_venues JOIN a count of check_ins in the last N days, ranked by (recent_checkin_count desc, distance asc). Call from Swift with supabase.rpc(). Send device location from CoreLocation; never trust client-declared location for anything security-sensitive.

MODERATION PIPELINE (MVP, cheap): (1) EULA + community-guidelines acceptance gate at signup; (2) report button on every check-in/photo/comment writing to reports; (3) block-user via friendships.status='blocked' that filters the feed both directions; (4) an Edge Function on new photo insert that calls a vision moderation API (e.g. Hive/AWS Rekognition/Google Vision SafeSearch) and sets is_flagged; flagged content is hidden pending human review; (5) a lightweight admin review view (web) reading via service role. Published support email in-app satisfies 1.2's contact requirement.

PUSH (MVP): request notification permission after first check-in (not at cold launch); store APNs token in device_tokens. Edge Function subscribes to DB webhooks (new follow, friend check-in nearby, badge earned) and POSTs to APNs with a .p8 JWT. Keep the .p8 key in Supabase secrets, never on-device. Use Live Activities later for 'friends out drinking now'.

IMAGES (MVP): upload check-in photos client-side directly to a private Supabase Storage bucket at path {user_id}/{check_in_id}.jpg (RLS matches storage.foldername(name)[1] = auth.uid()). Serve feed via signed URLs with transform width/quality for thumbnails; full-res on tap. Compress on-device (HEIC->JPEG ~1600px) before upload to cut egress.

SCALE ARCHITECTURE (post-PMF): (1) split read-heavy feed into a denormalized feed_items table (fan-out-on-write via Edge Function or pg trigger) instead of runtime joins; (2) move 'popular near me' + trending to a materialized view / scheduled pg_cron refresh, or precompute per H3/geohash cell; (3) add Redis/Upstash cache in front of hot geo queries; (4) introduce read replicas and pgBouncer connection pooling; (5) offload push fan-out and moderation to a queue (pgmq or external) with dedicated workers; (6) CDN in front of Storage; (7) full-text/vector search (pg_trgm or a dedicated search service) for beer/brewery discovery across a large catalog; (8) partition check_ins by month once it grows; (9) add Android via a second native app (Kotlin) or, if team constraints force it, evaluate Expo/RN at that point sharing only the Supabase backend.

OFFLINE: MVP = optimistic UI + a local queue (SwiftData/Core Data) for check-ins created without connectivity, synced on reconnect. Cache the beer/brewery catalog locally for scan-match lookups. Full offline-first sync (e.g. via a sync engine) is a scale-phase concern, not MVP.

LABEL SCAN: MVP = VisionKit DataScanner / Vision text recognition to read the beer name off a label/tap list, then fuzzy-match (pg_trgm similarity RPC) against the beers catalog; fall back to manual search. Barcode scanning (AVFoundation) for packaged beer UPC lookup. A trained image classifier is a later enhancement, not needed at MVP.
risks: App Store rejection under Guideline 1.4.3 if the app adds any purchase/order/delivery flow or reads as 'encouraging excessive consumption'. Mitigation: launch as discovery/logging only, add responsible-drinking messaging, no minor-targeted content. Any commerce is a deliberate later decision with legal review, and if added would need geo-restriction to legal jurisdictions (precedent: Guideline 5.1.1(ix) for cannabis).

Rejection under Guideline 1.2 if UGC moderation (filter + report + block + contact) is not fully present at first submission. This is the most likely non-alcohol rejection reason for a social app. Mitigation: ship all four before submitting; do not defer moderation to 'v1.1'.

Age-gating is declared-age (self-attested), which satisfies Apple but is weak against determined minors and offers limited legal protection in some jurisdictions. Mitigation: 17+ rating + gate + disclaimer at MVP; consider stronger verified-age only if regulation or a market demands it.

signInWithIdToken nonce handling is a common footgun: Apple needs the SHA256 hash of the nonce in the ASAuthorizationRequest while the RAW nonce goes to Supabase. Getting this backwards yields opaque 'invalid nonce' auth failures. Mitigation: implement per Supabase's documented pattern and test both providers early.

Client-reported geolocation is spoofable — do not gate rewards, leaderboards, or venue check-in badges purely on client lat/lng without sanity checks (velocity/plausibility), or 'popular near me' and badge systems can be gamed.

Push notification silent failures: a misconfigured APNs .p8 (wrong Team ID / Key ID / topic) returns success-looking responses while Apple drops the notification. Mitigation: validate against APNs sandbox, log APNs response IDs, monitor delivery.

Storage egress and image-transform cost can balloon with a photo-heavy feed. Mitigation: compress on-device, serve transformed thumbnails, CDN-cache, and set bucket size/type limits. Image transforms require Supabase Pro plan.

PostGIS geo queries without a GiST index (or querying on `geometry` instead of `geography`) degrade badly at scale and give wrong metric distances. Mitigation: use geography(Point,4326) + GiST index; verify EXPLAIN uses the index; precompute popularity at scale.

Choosing native Swift trades away cheap Android parity later. If Android becomes urgent, a second native codebase or a mid-project RN pivot is costly. Mitigation: keep ALL business logic in Supabase (RPCs, RLS, Edge Functions) so a future Android/RN client is a thin view layer over the same backend.
data.details:
FRAMEWORK DECISION — Native Swift/SwiftUI (iOS-first). Rationale mapped to the requested criteria: SPEED (iteration): RN/Expo edges native for hot-reload JS iteration, but Supabase-swift + SwiftUI previews are fast enough and the account already has an Apple Developer account and no Android requirement, so cross-platform buys nothing today. CROSS-PLATFORM: only a real advantage if Android is needed; it is not stated, so this is premature. PREMIUM FEEL: native wins clearly — ProMotion 120Hz scroll on a media feed, native gestures, haptics, Dynamic Island/Live Activities for social presence. LOCATION: CoreLocation gives precise foreground + background/geofence location with first-class permission UX; MapKit for venue maps. OFFLINE: SwiftData/Core Data local queue for check-ins. SOCIAL FEED: SwiftUI Lists/LazyVStack with native cell reuse handle image-heavy feeds smoothly. CAMERA/LABEL-SCAN: the decisive factor — VisionKit DataScanner, Vision text/barcode recognition, and AVFoundation are day-one at each WWDC; RN plugin wrappers lag 3–12 months. Per the 2026 comparisons, RN's New Architecture (mandatory since RN 0.82) closes the CRUD-perf gap but not the platform-API or polish gap, and Swift devs get new Apple APIs immediately. Keep 100% of business logic server-side (Postgres RPCs, RLS, Edge Functions) so any future Android/RN client is a thin layer.

AUTH FLOW (Supabase native, both providers). Sign in with Apple: generate a random rawNonce, SHA256-hash it, pass the hash as ASAuthorizationAppleIDRequest.nonce; on success take credential.identityToken (JWT) and call supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken(provider:.apple, idToken: identityToken, nonce: rawNonce). Google: GoogleSignIn-iOS returns result.user.idToken; call signInWithIdToken(provider:.google, idToken: idToken). Supabase verifies the JWT against Apple/Google public keys, creates or links the user by email, and returns a Supabase session (access + refresh token). Because the app is iOS-only, the Apple Services ID and web callback config are unnecessary. Offering Google + Apple satisfies Guideline 4.8 (Apple itself is the compliant equivalent login: name+email only, private-relay email, no ad tracking).

SCHEMA (see recommendations for column-level sketch). Core entities: profiles ↔ auth.users; breweries 1—* beers; venues (PostGIS point); check_ins as the central fact table (user × beer × optional venue, with rating, comment, photo, location, timestamp) — ratings live on the check-in rather than a separate table for MVP simplicity; badges + user_badges (earned via criteria jsonb evaluated in an Edge Function on check-in); flights + flight_items (ordered tasting lists); friendships (mutual, with pending/accepted/blocked) and follows (one-directional) modeled separately because a beer app typically wants both a symmetric friend graph and asymmetric following; reports and device_tokens for moderation and push. PostGIS enabled; geography(Point,4326) + GiST indexes on venues.location and check_ins.location. RLS on every table (policies in recommendations).

GEO / POPULAR NEAR ME. RPC nearby_venues(lat,lng,radius_m): WHERE ST_DWithin(location, ST_MakePoint(lng,lat)::geography, radius_m) ORDER BY ST_Distance(...). popular_near_me layers a JOIN to a count of check_ins within the same radius over a recent window (e.g. 7 days) and ranks by recent activity then distance. At scale, precompute popularity per geohash/H3 cell via pg_cron into a materialized view and cache hot cells.

IMAGES. Private Storage bucket, path {user_id}/{check_in_id}.jpg, RLS via storage.foldername(name)[1] = auth.uid(); on-device HEIC→JPEG compression before upload; signed-URL transforms for feed thumbnails (Pro plan) with full-res on tap; CDN at scale.

MODERATION (Guideline 1.2 — all four required at launch). Filter (EULA/community-guidelines gate + Edge-Function vision moderation setting check_ins.is_flagged, flagged content hidden pending review), Report (reports table + admin review via service role), Block (friendships.status='blocked' filtering the feed bidirectionally), Contact (published support email in-app).

PUSH. Supabase does not talk to APNs directly. Store per-install APNs token in device_tokens; an Edge Function subscribed to DB webhooks (new follow, friend check-in, badge earned) signs an APNs JWT with a .p8 key held in Supabase secrets and POSTs to APNs/HTTP2 — no Firebase needed. Use Live Activities later for real-time 'friends out now'.

APP STORE COMPLIANCE. Launch strictly as discovery/social/logging (no sale facilitation → clears 1.4.3); honest 17+ rating (2.3.6); in-app declared-age gate + responsible-drinking disclaimer; UGC moderation complete (1.2); Apple+Google login (4.8). Avoid any commerce; if ever added, geo-restrict to legal jurisdictions (precedent 5.1.1(ix)).

MVP vs SCALE. MVP = SwiftUI client + Supabase-only backend (Postgres/PostGIS + Auth + Storage + Realtime + a handful of Edge Functions), runtime geo/popularity queries, direct-to-APNs push, third-party vision moderation, optimistic-UI offline queue. SCALE = denormalized fan-out feed_items, materialized/pg_cron popularity per geo cell, Redis/Upstash cache, read replicas + pgBouncer, queue-based push/moderation workers (pgmq), Storage CDN, dedicated search (pg_trgm/vector) for the beer catalog, monthly partitioning of check_ins, and a native Kotlin Android app (or RN only if team constraints force it) reusing the identical Supabase backend.
sources: https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/ — App Store Review Guidelines: 1.4.3 (alcohol/tobacco/drugs, facilitating sale), 1.2 (UGC moderation: filter/report/block/contact), 4.8 (login service equivalence / Sign in with Apple), 2.3.6 (honest age rating), 1.2.1 (age-restriction mechanism), 5.1.1(ix) (geo-restriction precedent for controlled substances)
https://supabase.com/blog/native-mobile-auth — Native Mobile Auth Support for Google and Apple Sign in (signInWithIdToken, ID tokens, nonce handling, native vs OAuth redirect)
https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/social-login/auth-apple — Login with Apple (native iOS identityToken flow, hashed nonce, iOS-only skips Services ID)
https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/extensions/postgis — PostGIS geo queries (ST_DWithin, ST_Distance, geography type, sorting by distance)
https://postgis.net/docs/ST_DWithin.html — ST_DWithin reference (meters for geography, use_spheroid)
https://supabase.com/docs/guides/storage/serving/image-transformations — Storage image transformations (resize width/height/quality, signed-URL transforms, Pro plan requirement)

https://supabase.com/docs/guides/functions/examples/push-notifications — Sending Push Notifications with Edge Functions (store device token, trigger on insert, APNs/.p8 configuration; Supabase does not send APNs directly)
https://www.superappp.com/blog/native-swift-vs-react-native-expo-in-2025-2026 — Native Swift vs React Native (Expo) 2025-2026 comparison
https://studiokrew.com/blog/swift-vs-react-native-flutter-ios-2026/ — Swift vs React Native vs Flutter for iOS in 2026 (WWDC day-one API access, premium feel, camera/hardware integration)
https://www.applighter.com/blog/react-native-performance-benchmarks-expo-vs-bare-vs-flutter-vs-native-2026 — RN performance benchmarks 2026 (New Architecture startup times vs native)

assessments.name: Steiny
assessments.notes: No beer app or app-space trademark collision found (App Store 'steiny' returns only surname apps: David Stein, My Steinway Lyngdorf, etc.). BUT 'Steiny' is an established informal nickname for Steinlager (NZ lager, 1958) and echoes the 'steinie' beer-bottle style (Miller/Molson Coors heritage). There is also a real 'Steiny's Brewing' (Wallingford, CT). Risk is descriptive/nickname-based rather than a hard registered-mark clash: a big brewer (Lion/Kirin owns Steinlager) could object if the app leans into beer branding. Distinct enough to likely register, but not squeaky clean in the beer vertical.

assessments.name: Brewt
assessments.notes: No exact-name beer APP on the App Store (searching 'brewt' just fuzzy-matches generic beer apps: Untappd, Brewfather, BeerMenus). HOWEVER 'Brewt's LLC' is a real, active alcohol-adjacent brand: handcrafted cocktail mixers (Bloody Mary/Caesar, Margarita) + hot sauce, Grand Rapids MI, sold in bars/restaurants/stores across 26+ states, with LinkedIn/Crunchbase/Walmart presence. Same phonetics ('Brewt' vs 'Brewt's'), overlapping beverage/bar channel = real likelihood-of-confusion exposure for a drinks app. Also purely descriptive of 'brew,' which weakens distinctiveness and invites crowding.

assessments.name: Pilsy
assessments.notes: Cleanest of the set. No beer app, no brewery, no brand, and no trademark hits ('pilsy' returns unrelated foreign kids'/food apps on the App Store; web search finds only the pilsner category generally). Coined, ownable, and clearly beer-evocative (pils/pilsner) without copying any existing mark. Only mild caveat: it is suggestive of the 'pilsner' style, so a purely descriptive challenge is theoretically possible, but no competitor is using it.

assessments.name: Clink
assessments.notes: Heavily crowded in the exact drinks/social space. Multiple live App Store apps: 'Clink - NYC Happy Hour', 'Clink' by Clink Tech LLC (social/dating, id1575374800), plus near-identical siblings 'Clnk' (cocktail discovery app), 'Clinkly - Discover Happy Hours', and 'Clink Up' (Tampa happy-hour). Heineken also ran a 'The Clinker' cheers campaign. Direct name-and-category overlap makes both App Store discoverability and trademark clearance very difficult.

assessments.name: Froth
assessments.notes: No beer app named 'Froth' on the App Store, but the name is contested: App Store 'froth' returns coffee apps (Froth Coffee & Dessert, Froth Espresso Cafe, Froth - The Coffee Bar) and a social app simply called 'Froth' (Stephen Espiritu). In beer specifically there are at least two real 'Froth Brewing' companies (Froth Brewing Co., Buffalo NY, and Froth Brewing Company). Generic, widely-used beverage descriptor = crowded field and weak distinctiveness, though no single dominant beer-app owner.

assessments.name: Suds
assessments.notes: App Store 'suds' is dominated by laundry/car-wash apps (Suds Car Wash, Suds App detailing, multiple laundromat apps) — so the exact term is heavily used, just mostly outside beer. In beer it is a generic slang word for beer itself ('grab some suds'), which makes it descriptive and hard to own/protect, and there are real-world 'Suds' beer stores/brews on Untappd. Low legal-conflict-with-a-beer-app risk, but poor distinctiveness and cross-category App Store clutter (laundry) hurt both trademark strength and search discoverability.

assessments.name: Tappr
assessments.notes: Direct hit: 'TAPPr - The Ultimate Homebrewer's Companion' (tappr.beer) is an existing beer/homebrew app (keg tracking, tasting notes, Brefather sync, launching iOS/Android). Also collides conceptually and phonetically with the whole tap-list category (TapHunter, Taplist.io, Untappd). Same name, same vertical, imminent/active app = clear likelihood of confusion. Avoid.

assessments.name: Nectar
assessments.notes: Crowded across the alcohol/drinks space with multiple established players: 'Nectar' hard seltzer brand (BevMo-listed, 4.7% ABV), 'Nectar Imports' (Asahi-owned UK drinks supplier with its own consumer ordering app on iOS/Android), and 'Nectar' bar-inventory system (getnectar.com, smart pour-tracking caps for bars). Multiple live alcohol-industry trademarks and at least one existing drinks app = high conflict for a new beer app.

assessments.name: Pinta
assessments.notes: Strongly contested in beer. 'Browar PINTA' is Poland's largest craft brewery (est. 2011, well-known IPAs). 'Pinta Libre' is an existing subscription beer app operating across Argentina/Spain/Latin America. 'Pinter/Pinta' home-brewing device + 'Pinter App' (World Microbrewery of the Year 2025) is phonetically nearly identical and directly in the beer-app space. 'Pinta' also literally means 'pint' in Spanish/Italian (descriptive for beer). App Store 'pinta' returns unrelated paint apps, but the beer-world collisions and descriptiveness make this high risk.

assessments.name: Hoply
assessments.notes: No beer/alcohol app named 'Hoply' (App Store returns a doc viewer, a running-coach app, and a travel app), and no brewery/brand called exactly 'Hoply.' BUT it is one letter off from a very crowded beer cluster built on 'hop': 'Hoppy - AI-Powered Beer Search' (App Store id1406526158), 'Hoppy - Beer Knowledge' by AB InBev, 'Hopfully Brewing Co.', 'Hopularity'. High phonetic/conceptual similarity to Hoppy (an active beer app) creates real likelihood-of-confusion and App Store discoverability risk despite the exact string being free.

assessments.name: Cheerz
assessments.notes: Multiple live drink apps already use it: 'Cheerz by MFA' (App Store id6755335680) is a drink-tracking app for nights out (track beers, badges, leaderboard) — nearly the same concept a new beer app would have — and a second 'Cheerz' offers free-drink venue rewards. Also collides with 'Cheers! Fun Beer Drinking Game', the 'Cheers' social cocktail app, and a photo brand 'Cheerz.' Same category, same name = high conflict and poor App Store differentiation.

nameVerify.details:
Method: For each candidate I ran (1) a web search for beer/brewery/alcohol brands and trademarks, and (2) a direct Apple App Store search (itunes.apple.com/search) to see what apps actually ship under or near the name. I weighted collisions inside the beer/drinks vertical far more heavily than unrelated categories (e.g., laundry apps for 'Suds', paint apps for 'Pinta').

TIERS:
LOW (essentially clean) — Pilsy: no app, brand, brewery, or mark found anywhere; coined and beer-evocative. Clearly the safest and most ownable.

MEDIUM (registrable but with a caveat) — Steiny (nickname for Steinlager + 'steinie' bottle style, no app collision), Hoply (one letter from the active 'Hoppy' beer apps), Brewt (no beer app, but real alcohol-adjacent brand 'Brewt's LLC' cocktail mixers in bars nationwide; also descriptive of 'brew'), Froth (coffee apps + a social 'Froth' app + real Froth breweries; generic), Suds (generic beer slang, weak distinctiveness; App Store cluttered with laundry/car-wash 'Suds').

HIGH (avoid / very hard to clear) — Cheerz (multiple live drink-tracking/rewards apps), Nectar (hard-seltzer brand + Asahi's Nectar Imports app + Nectar bar-inventory system), Clink (Clink NYC Happy Hour, Clink dating, Clnk, Clinkly, Clink Up — saturated), Tappr (an actual homebrew app 'TAPPr' at tappr.beer, plus the whole tap-list category), Pinta (Browar PINTA brewery, Pinta Libre beer app, Pinter/Pinta home-brew app; also literally means 'pint').

Recommendation: Pilsy is the standout clean pick. Steiny and Hoply are viable with counsel doing a formal USPTO/EUIPO clearance (Steiny should avoid Steinlager-adjacent branding; Hoply should be distanced visually from 'Hoppy'). Everything from Brewt down carries meaningful conflict or distinctiveness problems, and Tappr, Pinta, Clink, Nectar, and Cheerz should be dropped for a beer app. NOTE: this is not legal advice — a licensed trademark attorney should run a formal clearance search (USPTO TESS/EUIPO) plus a full App Store name-availability check before filing or launch.

synthesis.concept: Pilsy is a free, global, scan-first beer passport — point your camera at any label, tap list, or barcode and instantly get a personalized, style-fair match score, log the pour to your Cellar, and see what's actually good on tap near you. Every capability the incumbents lock behind a paywall — rating precision, discovery, an ad-free feed, brewery menus — Pilsy gives away for life and earns instead on the supply side, so drinkers pay nothing and breweries fund the party.
name_shortlist: Pilsy (low collision risk — verification's cleanest; coined + ownable; lager connotation is a marketing problem, not a legal one)
Steiny (medium — strongest brand/gamification fit but Steinlager nickname collision; primary fallback)
Hoply (medium — clearly beer, exact string free, but one letter off live 'Hoppy' app)
Brewt (medium — invented + ownable, but real 'Brewt's' cocktail-mixer brand in the same channel)
synthesis.stack_recommendation: Native Swift 6 / SwiftUI (iOS 18+) with a Supabase backend (Postgres + PostGIS + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions). No separate app server at MVP.
synthesis.stack_rationale: Every Pilsy differentiator lands exactly where native wins in 2026: label/barcode scanning (the hero) uses VisionKit DataScanner + AVFoundation which ship day-one at WWDC while RN wrappers lag 3–12 months; a fluid image-heavy social feed benefits from SwiftUI + ProMotion 120Hz; premium feel comes from Dynamic Island / Live Activities / haptics; CoreLocation gives precise + background/geofence location and MapKit is free for venue maps; SwiftData handles offline check-ins at tailgates and the back nine. RN's New Architecture closed the CRUD-perf gap but not the platform-integration or polish gap. The account already ships native Swift (Lore, Nalee) with an Apple Developer account and no stated Android deadline, so cross-platform is premature optimization. Critical rule: keep 100% of business logic server-side (Postgres RPCs, RLS, Edge Functions) so a future Kotlin Android client — or RN only if team constraints force it — is a thin view layer over the identical backend. Supabase matches existing Nalee/Lore muscle memory; PostGIS powers 'popular near me' via geography(Point,4326) + GiST index + ST_DWithin/ST_Distance; auth is Sign in with Apple + Google via signInWithIdToken (test the nonce SHA256-hash footgun early); push is a direct APNs .p8 JWT from an Edge Function, no Firebase.
mvp_features: Scan → identify → rate: VisionKit/AVFoundation reads label/barcode/tap-list, fuzzy-matches (pg_trgm) to catalog, manual-search fallback — the hero loop
Style-normalized + personalized 'predicted-for-you' score — the reason to trust Pilsy where Untappd is distrusted
The Cellar — personal collection + wishlist where every pour is logged
Log a Pour (check-in) with optional photo, flavor-tag chips, glassware, venue, rating — the atomic action
Tap List / 'On Near You' — nearby breweries & bars (Foursquare + Apple POI) plus 'popular beers near me' from our own check-ins
The Passport — styles-explored map + country stamps; breadth-based gamification, never volume
Curiosity-weighted badges (explorer/style/country) — no ABV, quantity, daily-streak, or morning-drinking badges
Friends + feed — follow/friend graph where badge unlocks and hauls drive discovery
No/Low lens — togglable across the whole app; NA & sub-3.5% beers count fully toward the Passport; day-one, not bolted-on
One-tap import of Untappd/RateBeer personal history (data-portability hook, not scraping their ratings graph)
Age gate + responsible-drinking design: 17+, declared-age gate, disclaimer
UGC moderation — all four required at first submission: content filter, report, block-user, published contact
later_features: Flights — curated guided tasting quests (IPA Journey, NA All-Stars, Around the Belgian Table); completing one = badge + map progress
Crew / Live Session — flagship social differentiator: real-time shared group check-in for a tailgate, party, or golf scramble with live leaderboard + photo wall (Untappd has nothing like it)
On-course mode for golfers — hole/course tagging, combined 'scramble scorecard + beer tally,' offline sync for dead zones
Brewery Pro portal — verified profile, 'On Tap Now' manager, live check-in analytics, event/release push (the durable revenue engine)
Real tap-list ingestion via UTFB/POS partnership (not scraping)
Where-to-buy / delivery affiliate — commerce garnish, geo-restricted
Brewery Bingo, bottle-share events, homebrew recipe logging, merch
Android (native Kotlin) reusing the identical Supabase backend

segment_hooks.hook: Crew / Live Session — a shared party or tailgate check-in with a group leaderboard and photo wall, turning logging into a bonding ritual instead of a solo diary. Retains via friends already on it; churns on any signup/age-wall friction at a party or anything that reads as pushing binge. Lean light lagers, fruity, and NA.

On-course mode — hole/course tagging plus a combined 'scramble scorecard + beer tally' for the foursome, with offline check-ins that sync. This is whitespace between beer apps and golf apps (18Birdies tracks games but no beer). Churns without offline support on the back nine.

The Cellar plus a deep, trustworthy, style-fair database — rich collection, rare-release alerts, later homebrew logging. Retention hinges on data completeness and de-dup (bad data is their #1 churn driver); churns on shallow ratings or over-simplification that dilutes their serious tool.

One-tap scan plus 'you liked X, try Y nearby' and a genuinely useful No/Low surface — remember a good beer with zero homework. Retains via frictionlessness and friends' pours; churns on ticker-culture intimidation or feeling judged for drinking little or picking NA.

Free claimed profile upgrading to a Pro portal — 'On Tap Now,' live check-in analytics, and event/release push to nearby matched drinkers, radically cheaper than Untappd with real data ownership. Retention needs local drinker density (no check-ins = no value); churns on thin density, clunky tooling, or fees a small taproom can't justify.

data_sources: Foursquare OS Places (100M+ POIs, Apache 2.0 — owned & warehoused, no per-call fees) for the bar/brewery POI layer
Open Brewery DB (free, no key, effectively public-domain) for brewery-specific enrichment
Open Food Facts (barcode → beer + brand + label image, ODbL + CC-BY-SA — attribute & segregate) to seed the beer catalog and scan-to-add
Punk API community rehost (415 beers) for demo/seed polish only

First-party user check-ins — the ONLY compliant source of 'popular beers near me': (venue from Foursquare/Apple POI) + (beer from our catalog) + (rating from our users), ranked by recent check-in count × recency-decay × avg rating within ST_DWithin
Apple MapKit (free, iOS-native) and Google Places (place_id only — no caching) for live venue enrichment/verification only, never warehoused

Cold-start bootstrap: editorial staff-picks / style-default empty states + first-check-in incentives + metro-by-metro density seeding (campus, golf club, brewery cluster); NOT importing Untappd ratings (ToS violation + poisons provenance)
monetization: Phase 1 (0–10k MAU/metro): free brewery profiles to build the check-in habit + events/festival ticketing affiliate (Eventbrite/DICE deep-links + promo-code splits) — the cleanest line legally

Phase 2 (10–50k, flagship): brewery/taproom partnerships — verified profile, 'Featured' placement, self-updated 'On Tap Now'; tiered self-serve ads (~$49–99 Verified, ~$199–299 Premium) priced under Untappd's $1,199/yr
Phase 2: B2B Pro tier sold only to breweries (~$99–299/mo) — analytics, menu/tap-list mgmt, review-response, campaign manager, competitor benchmarking; the durable, highest-LTV recurring engine
Phase 3 (50k+): sponsored badges & challenges, platform-run ($299–999/campaign) — proven Untappd revenue line

Phase 3: alcohol e-commerce/delivery affiliate as garnish, not a pillar (ReserveBar ~4%, Athletic ~3%, Beerwulf 6–12%; Drizly dead, Instacart excludes alcohol, DoorDash/Uber have no public consumer deep-link) — negligible under ~100k MAU
Phase 3: merch via print-on-demand (breweries sell via profile for a platform cut) — legally cleanest since it's not alcohol

Compliance rails: 21+ gate before any purchase CTA; TTB mandatory-statement link allowance; keep manufacturer $ and retailer $ in separate lanes (tied-house 'thing of value' is the biggest legal risk); geo-suppress commerce in ban states (Utah, Delaware) + dry counties; FTC affiliate disclosure

brand.voice: The knowledgeable friend at the bar who's genuinely thrilled you're curious — never the snob quizzing you. Fun, warm, worldly, a little witty. Frat-friendly (playful, group energy) but never frat-exclusive (never crude, never 'bro,' never gatekeeping). Celebrates curiosity over capacity — hypes trying something new, not drinking a lot. Global by default, NA drinkers first-class. Two register levels: plain-language default with a toggle-on 'Beer-geek mode' that swaps in the lexicon (Tick a Pour, The Cellar, Whales, Haul). Sample microcopy: empty state 'Your Cellar's looking thirsty'; NA lens 'Big flavor, zero proof. These count just as much.'
palette: #F2A900 Pour Gold — primary brand core (lager/pilsner gold), CTAs, active states, stein/passport progress fill
#1A1206 Malt Black — text, dark surfaces, premium roasty backdrop
#FBF6EC Foam — warm off-white background (the head on a pour, not clinical white)
#3F8F5B Fresh Hop — the No/Low lens, 'fresh on tap,' success states; signals inclusivity not IPA
#B4531F Copper Ale — amber/dark-style accents, whales/rare finds, celebration moments
#6B6459 Slate — secondary text, dividers, disabled states
brand.type_direction: Display/wordmark: friendly, slightly-rounded geometric sans with warmth and confidence (Poppins/General-Sans register) — approachable and global-legible, never craft-blackletter cliché. Body/UI: clean neutral sans (Inter-class) for scannable feeds and dense beer data. Pick faces with clear tabular figures — ratings, ABV, IBU, and country counts are everywhere.
brand.logo_concept: A stein/pint silhouette whose foam head doubles as a passport stamp / location pin — one mark fusing the three pillars (a beer, a place, a journey). Pour Gold on Foam; the foam-line animates as the app's progress/fill motif (Passport filling, badge unlocking). Simple enough at 60×60 app-icon size, distinctive enough to own. A secondary 'stamp' glyph (the foam-pin alone) serves as the check-in/achievement mark.

risks: Cold-start / network effects (dominant execution risk): POI + catalog + images seed the map but 'popular near me' starts at zero ratings and looks empty until local density exists — only metro-by-metro growth + first-check-in incentives fix it, not the data plan

Two-sided chicken-and-egg on revenue: brewery partnerships and Pro both need pre-existing density to sell, so revenue is back-loaded and runway must cover a long free period (~10k MAU/metro before there's anything to monetize)

Apple rejection under 1.4.3 (any purchase/order flow or 'encourages excessive consumption' read) and 1.2 (must ship filter+report+block+contact at first submission — the most likely non-alcohol rejection reason for a social app)

Rating credibility cuts both ways: the style-normalized score is the moat but if it feels gameable or like a black box it inherits Untappd's exact distrust, and breweries fear low scores tanking sales — keep it explainable + add location plausibility checks

Big-beer / incumbent retaliation: AB InBev killed RateBeer; Next Glass owns Untappd AND BeerAdvocate and could clone a free tier or cut business pricing — the moat must be trust + UX + commerce rails, not just 'free'
Mindful-drinking trend cuts both ways: the NA wave that widens the TAM also shrinks the heavy-check-in, high-commission core — design for breadth and NA-first, not the shrinking hardcore ticker base

Commerce is regulated and thin: alcohol e-commerce is a state-by-state patchwork (three-tier, DTC bans, age verification) and affiliate rates are low, so 'global + commission' earns nothing until conversion happens and 'global' multiplies the legal burden — treat commerce as a deliberate, geo-restricted, later decision

Beer-identity de-dup is genuinely hard: if the same beer splits across 5 spellings it never trends and the hero feature silently dies — budget real engineering + a human merge queue with barcode/GTIN as the strongest key
synthesis.brief_markdown:
# Pilsy — Product Brief

**A free, global, scan-first beer passport. Untappd's fun, without the tax.**

---

## 1. Hero Concept

**Pilsy is a free, global, scan-first beer passport — point your camera at any label, tap list, or barcode and instantly get a personalized, style-fair match score, log the pour to your Cellar, and see what's actually good on tap near you.** Every capability the incumbents lock behind a paywall — rating precision, discovery, an ad-free feed, brewery menus — Pilsy gives away for life and earns instead on the supply side, so drinkers pay nothing and breweries fund the party.

One-line pitch: **Vivino's scan-and-go, rebuilt for beer, on data we own — trustworthy where Untappd is distrusted, generous where it gouges, and global and no/low-first where it's defensive.**

---

## 2. The Wedge (why this wins now)

- **RateBeer is dead** (AB InBev shut it Feb 2025). **BeerAdvocate** is consolidated under Untappd's parent (Next Glass) and stagnant — the encyclopedic niche is depleted and its users are loose.
- **Untappd** is 2.3/5 on Trustpilot: ad-heavy, buggy, paywalls precision (Insiders $5.99/mo), doubled business price to $1,199/yr with no grandfathering, and made a resented late-2025 land-grab beyond beer. Its ratings are structurally distrusted — raters are <1% of drinkers who 1-star beers for being the *wrong style*.
- **Vivino** proved the model (70M downloads, 2.7B scans, ~$489M GMV, ~15% take) but only for wine.
- Long-tail apps each do **one** job (BeerMenus = tap lists, Tavour = drops, Pintley = taste recs). **No single app unifies trustworthy taste-match + discovery + where-to-get-it + purchase, for free, globally.** That gap is the wedge.

---

## 3. Name

**Recommendation: `Pilsy`** — the cleanest name in verification (the only "low" collision risk in the set: no app, brewery, brand, or trademark hit). Coined, ownable, warm, beer-evocative, easy worldwide. The one caveat — "pils" leans lager — is a *marketing* problem, not a legal one, solved by style-neutral positioning ("your whole beer world, one passport"). Clean clearance means fastest, safest path to an App Store title and a filed mark.

### Shortlist (ranked by verification cleanliness)

| Name | Risk | Why it's on the list / caveat |
|---|---|---|
| **Pilsy** | Low | Cleanest overall; coined + ownable. Neutralize the lager read in brand copy. |
| **Steiny** | Medium | Best *brand* fit — style-neutral, stein = native gamification currency. Caveat: Steinlager nickname; avoid Steinlager-adjacent visuals. **Primary fallback.** |
| **Hoply** | Medium | Clearly beer (hops), exact string free. Caveat: one letter off live "Hoppy"; distance it. |
| **Brewt** | Medium | Invented, ownable. Caveat: real "Brewt's" cocktail-mixer brand in the same channel. |

**Dropped** (high risk): Clink, Tappr, Nectar, Pinta, Cheerz, Froth, Suds.

> **Before commit:** formal USPTO/EUIPO clearance (Nice classes 9 + 42) + domain checks (`pilsy.app`, `pilsy.com`, `getpilsy.com`).

---

## 4. Tech Stack

**Recommendation: native Swift 6 / SwiftUI (iOS 18+), Supabase backend.** No separate app server at MVP.

### Swift vs Expo

Every differentiator lands where native wins in 2026, and the account already ships native Swift (Lore, Nalee) with an Apple Developer account and **no Android deadline** — so cross-platform is premature optimization.

| Differentiator | Why native Swift wins |
|---|---|
| **Label / barcode scan** (hero) | VisionKit DataScanner + AVFoundation ship day-one at WWDC; RN wrappers lag 3–12 months. Decisive. |
| **Fluid social feed** | SwiftUI + ProMotion 120Hz on image-heavy lists. |
| **Premium feel** | Dynamic Island, Live Activities ("friends out now"), haptics. |
| **Location** | CoreLocation precise + geofence; MapKit (free) maps. |
| **Offline** | SwiftData local queue for the tailgate / back nine. |

RN's New Architecture closed the *CRUD-perf* gap, not the *platform/polish* gap. **Rule: keep 100% of business logic server-side (Postgres RPCs, RLS, Edge Functions) so a future Kotlin Android client — or RN only if forced — is a thin view layer over the identical backend.**

### Backend

- **Supabase** (Postgres + **PostGIS** + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions) — Nalee/Lore muscle memory.
- **Auth**: Sign in with Apple + Google via `signInWithIdToken`. *Footgun to test early:* Apple needs the **SHA256 hash** of the nonce in the request; the **raw** nonce goes to Supabase.
- **Geo / "popular near me"**: `geography(Point,4326)` + GiST index; a `SECURITY DEFINER` RPC using `ST_DWithin` + `ST_Distance`, joined to a recent check-in count, ranked by `recent_checkins × recency-decay × avg_rating`.
- **Push**: APNs `.p8` JWT direct from an Edge Function on DB webhook — no Firebase.
- **Storage**: private bucket, RLS keyed to `auth.uid()`; on-device HEIC→JPEG compression; signed-URL thumbnails.

---

## 5. The Trust Fix (the moat)

Untappd's deepest flaw is a score nobody believes — a Kölsch punished against Double IPAs. Pilsy's core differentiator:

- **Style-normalized score** — rate each beer *relative to its own style*.
- **Predicted-for-YOU match** — a flavor graph + taste model surfaces "you'll probably love this."
- **Untappd structurally cannot retrofit this** without invalidating 15 years of legacy numbers.

Guardrail: keep it explainable ("we normalized within *Pilsner*"), never a black box, or it inherits the exact distrust it's meant to solve.

---

| # | Feature | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Scan → identify → rate** | VisionKit/AVFoundation; `pg_trgm` fuzzy-match; manual fallback. The hero. |
| 2 | **Style-fair + personalized score** | The reason to trust Pilsy. |

## 7. Later Features (post-PMF, sequenced)

1. **Flights** — curated guided tasting quests; completing one = badge + map progress.
2. **Crew / Live Session** — flagship social differentiator: real-time shared group check-in for a tailgate, party, or scramble with live leaderboard + photo wall.
3. **On-course mode** — hole/course tagging, "scramble scorecard + beer tally," offline sync.
4. **Brewery Pro portal** — verified profile, "On Tap Now," live analytics, event/release push. Durable revenue engine.
5. **Sponsored badges, challenges, passports/crawls** — platform-run.
6. **Real tap-list ingestion** via UTFB/POS *partnership*, not scraping.
7. **Where-to-buy / delivery affiliate** — commerce garnish.
8. **Brewery Bingo, bottle-share events, homebrew logging, merch.**
9. **Android** (native Kotlin) on the identical backend.

## 8. Per-Segment Hooks

| Segment | Killer hook | Retains / churns |
|---|---|---|
| **College / frat / tailgaters** | **Crew / Live Session** — shared party check-in with group leaderboard + photo wall; logging becomes a bonding ritual. | Retains: friends on it. Churns: signup/age-wall friction at a party; anything that reads as pushing binge. Lean light lagers, fruity, NA. |
| **Golfers** | **On-course mode** — hole/course tagging + "scramble scorecard + beer tally"; offline sync. Whitespace vs golf apps (18Birdies tracks games, not beer). | Churns without back-nine offline support. |
| **Enthusiasts / tickers / homebrewers** | **The Cellar + a deep, trustworthy DB** — style-fair scores, rare-release alerts, later homebrew logging. | Retains: data completeness + de-dup. Churns: shallow ratings; over-simplification. |
| **Casual drinkers** | **One-tap scan + "you liked X, try Y nearby"** + a real No/Low surface. Zero homework. | Retains: frictionlessness + friends' pours. Churns: ticker intimidation; feeling judged for NA. |
| **Breweries / taprooms** | **Free profile → Pro portal**: "On Tap Now," live analytics, event push to nearby matched drinkers — cheaper than Untappd, real data ownership. | Retains: local density. Churns: thin density, clunky tooling, fees. |

**Connective tissue:** friends graph + shared occasions. Enthusiasts build the trustworthy DB; casual/students consume recs via friends and Crew; golfers/tailgaters generate group occasions; breweries convert demand into events — closing the loop that funds "free."

## 9. Data: "Popular Beers Near Me" + Cold Start

**Non-negotiable truth: no compliant third-party API sells you "top beers pouring near this venue."** Untappd's API *forbids* the use case (Terms 5/13/14/15) and rate-limits it (100/hr). Google Places bans caching anything but `place_id`. **The hero metric must be a derived signal over first-party data we own.**

### How "popular near me" is powered

`(venue from Foursquare/Apple POI) + (beer from our catalog) + (rating from OUR users)`, ranked by
`recent check-in count × recency-decay × avg rating` within `ST_DWithin(location, radius)` — Untappd's mechanic rebuilt on data we own, zero ToS exposure.

### Cold-start in three moves

| Layer | Source | License / note |
|---|---|---|
| **POI (bars/breweries)** | **Foursquare OS Places** (100M+) + **Open Brewery DB** | **Apache 2.0** — owned & warehoused, no per-call fees. OBDB effectively public-domain. |
| **Beer catalog + labels** | **Open Food Facts** (barcode → beer + image) + **Punk API** (415-beer demo) | ODbL + CC-BY-SA — **attribute + segregate** so share-alike doesn't infect proprietary tables. |
| **Ratings** | **Start at zero — bootstrap it.** | Editorial "staff picks" empty states; incentivize first check-ins; seed density **metro-by-metro** (campus, golf club, brewery cluster). |

**Hard rules:** never persist Google content except `place_id`; never build a catalog from Untappd's API; keep provenance IDs (`fsq_id`, `obdb_id`, `off_barcode`, `google_place_id`); barcode/GTIN is the strongest natural key; budget engineering + a human merge queue for **beer-identity de-dup** — split spellings never trend and the hero feature silently dies.

**Live enrichment only** (never warehoused): Google Places / Apple MapKit for on-demand verification.

## 10. Monetization — free to users, forever

**Users never pay. Every dollar is supply-side.** Copy Vivino (monetize commerce), invert Untappd (don't paywall the community). Sequenced against density — the two best lines only sell once density exists.

| Phase | Line | Mechanics | Activates at |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1 (0–10k MAU/metro)** | Free profiles + **events ticketing affiliate** | Profiles build the check-in habit; in-app festival calendar with "Get Tickets" deep-links + promo-code splits. Cleanest legally. | Day one. |
| **2 (10–50k)** | **Brewery partnerships** (flagship) + **Pro tier** | Verified/"Featured"/self-updated "On Tap Now"; tiered ads (~$49–99 / ~$199–299) **under Untappd's $1,199/yr**. Pro adds analytics + menu mgmt (~$99–299/mo). | ~5k–25k MAU/metro. |
| **3 (50k+)** | **Sponsored badges** → **merch** + **affiliate** | Platform-run badges ($299–999/campaign). Affiliate ("Buy / Find delivery") is **garnish, not a pillar.** | Post-50k; affiliate negligible <100k. |

**Affiliate reality:** Drizly dead; Instacart excludes alcohol; DoorDash/Uber have no public deep-link. Only low-rate e-commerce pays (ReserveBar ~4%, Athletic ~3%, Beerwulf 6–12%). Volume play, never the main course.

**Compliance rulebook (day one):** 21+ gate before any purchase CTA · TTB mandatory-statement link allowance · **manufacturer $ and retailer $ in separate lanes** — neutral published-rate placement, never pay-to-bury (tied-house "thing of value" is the biggest legal risk) · geo-suppress commerce in ban states (Utah, Delaware) + dry counties · FTC disclosure.

## 11. Brand Direction

**Voice — the knowledgeable friend at the bar who's genuinely thrilled you're curious, never the snob quizzing you.** Fun, warm, worldly, a little witty. Frat-*friendly* but never frat-*exclusive* (never crude, never "bro," never gatekeeping). Celebrate **curiosity over capacity**. Global by default; NA drinkers first-class. Two registers: plain-language default with a toggle-on **"Beer-geek mode"** (Tick a Pour, The Cellar, Whales, Haul).

*Microcopy:* empty state → *"Your Cellar's looking thirsty."* · NA lens → *"Big flavor, zero proof. These count just as much."* · badge → *"New stamp! Beers from 12 countries. Where next?"*

### Palette

| Role | Hex | Use |
|---|---|---|
| **Pour Gold** (primary) | `#F2A900` | Brand core; CTAs, active states, stein/passport fill. |
| **Malt Black** (ink) | `#1A1206` | Text, dark surfaces, roasty backdrop. |
| **Foam** (surface) | `#FBF6EC` | Warm off-white background — the head on a pour. |
| **Fresh Hop** (secondary) | `#3F8F5B` | The No/Low lens, "fresh on tap," success. |
| **Copper Ale** (accent) | `#B4531F` | Amber accents, whales/rare finds, celebration. |
| **Slate** (muted) | `#6B6459` | Secondary text, dividers, disabled. |

Warm and appetizing — deliberately **not** the cold blue-grey of a data app.

### Type direction

- **Display / wordmark:** friendly, slightly-rounded geometric sans (Poppins / General-Sans register) — approachable, global-legible, no craft-blackletter cliché.
- **Body / UI:** clean neutral sans (Inter-class) for scannable feeds and dense beer data.
- **Numerals matter:** ratings, ABV, IBU, country counts everywhere — clear tabular figures.

### Logo concept

A **stein/pint silhouette whose foam head doubles as a passport stamp / location pin** — one mark fusing the three pillars (a beer, a place, a journey). Pour Gold on Foam; the foam-line animates as the app's progress/fill motif. Reads at 60×60 app-icon size, distinctive enough to own. A secondary "stamp" glyph (foam-pin alone) is the check-in/achievement mark.

## 12. Top Risks

1. **Cold-start / network effects (dominant).** POI + catalog + images seed the map, but "popular near me" starts at **zero ratings** and looks empty until density exists. Only metro-by-metro growth + first-check-in incentives fix it.
2. **Two-sided chicken-and-egg on revenue.** Partnerships and Pro both need pre-existing density; revenue is back-loaded — runway must cover a long free period.
3. **Apple rejection (1.4.3 + 1.2).** No purchase flow / "excessive consumption" read; ship social/logging-only + all four UGC-moderation pieces at first submission.
4. **Rating credibility cuts both ways.** The style-normalized score is the moat but must stay explainable and hard to game (location plausibility checks); breweries fear low scores.
5. **Big-beer / incumbent retaliation.** AB InBev killed RateBeer; Next Glass owns Untappd *and* BeerAdvocate. Moat = trust + UX + commerce rails, not just "free."
6. **Mindful-drinking trend cuts both ways.** The NA wave widens the TAM but shrinks the heavy-check-in core — design for breadth and NA-first.
7. **Commerce is regulated and thin.** State-by-state patchwork + low affiliate rates; treat commerce as a deliberate, geo-restricted, later decision.
